title: Risk Register Row
description: One activity-level row of the Project Risk Register — the minimum project control benchmark. Broader than the HRCW Register: covers HRCW and non-HRCW risks. Controls are tagged with hierarchy levels so residual-risk realism can be checked deterministically.

ref.description: Phase-prefixed row ref (e.g. SE-01, BF-03, TP-01A).

trade_package.description: Canonical package name for this risk row. Must be an exact member of project.trade_packages.

risk_status.description: Whether this is confirmed, conditional, latent, not applicable or a scope exclusion.

scope_status.description: Whether the activity is active scope, conditional scope, excluded scope or stop-work referral only.

phase.description: Construction phase header (e.g. '1 — SITE ESTABLISHMENT'). Rows are ordered in construction sequence.

hazard.description: The material hazard. Include only hazards materially created by this package — not generic construction hazards.

hrcw_categories.description: Confirmed HRCW categories engaged by this row. Empty when non_hrcw = true or the trigger is conditional only.

conditional_hrcw_categories.description: Conditional HRCW categories that apply only if the final method or site condition confirms the trigger.

non_hrcw.description: True for genuine non-HRCW risks (manual handling, thermal, dust/RCS alone, QA). RCS dust alone is non-HRCW and must not be coded H12.

latent_condition.description: True where the risk is possible but not planned (suspected ACM, hidden live services, unexpected ground). Controls must use stop-work / isolate / assess / revised-method logic.

classification_tags.description: Machine-readable risk classifications used by Phase 3 validators. Empty only where no listed classification applies.

initial_risk.description: Risk before controls. High(3) / Medium(2) / Low(1).

controls.description: Minimum-standard controls. Each carries the hierarchy level(s) it represents, enabling deterministic residual checks.

source_ids.description: Source control ID(s) from knowledge/control_library.json selected for this control.

text.description: The control statement. Must state what is controlled, how, when, who verifies, and what happens if missing. Weak patterns ('use safe work practices', bare 'wear PPE', 'follow SWMS') are rejected.

control_status.description: Whether this control is active, conditional, stop-work, supporting-document based or not applicable.

value_text.description: The fixed numeric limit stated in the control text.

basis.description: Machine-readable basis for the numeric limit.

basis_reference.description: Optional source reference, such as drawing, manufacturer instruction, standard clause or site rule.

fixed_numeric_limits.description: Required by Phase 3 validators when the control text contains a fixed numeric limit.

residual_risk.description: Risk after controls. May reduce only where controls materially change exposure — never to Low on administrative/PPE controls alone for a high-energy activity.

residual_justification.description: Why the residual rating is credible. Required where high_energy = true (so a reduction is not asserted without basis).

high_energy.description: True for fall from height, suspended load, mobile-plant interaction, temporary-works reliance, unverified roof access, openings, large public/occupied interface, high-energy plant/services. Triggers the residual-realism and exclusion-zone validators.

fall_arrest_reliant.description: True where the control relies on fall arrest. Requires rescue_readiness and residual no lower than Medium.

rescue_readiness.description: Rescue-from-height readiness statement. Required when fall_arrest_reliant = true.

exclusion_or_segregation.description: True where an exclusion zone or physical segregation control is present. Required when high_energy = true.

responsible_do.description: Primary accountable role that implements the work ('Do:'). A real role, never 'all personnel'.

swms_title.description: Governing SWMS title, or '—' where none. Must match the SWMS Matrix title.

hold_points.description: Hold point refs named in this row. Every ref must exist in the Hold Point Schedule.

basis_refs.description: Legal or code reference IDs supporting the risk row.

source_fact_refs.description: Project-fact IDs or approved source refs supporting confirmed risk/scope status.

confirmation_item_refs.description: Confirmation item IDs that may change this risk, scope, HRCW or control status.

supporting_document_refs.description: Supporting document IDs linked to this risk row.

override_allowed.description: True only where a professional/audited override is permitted for this row.

override_reason.description: Audit reason for an override decision.

approved_by.description: Person or role approving the override.

source_or_basis.description: Evidence or source basis for the override.
